surface.name: Pipedrive API-token first request through cURL
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, the fastest documented Pipedrive route is the direct cURL API-token request: no local or OSS CRM route exists, and cURL avoids the Postman installation required by the dedicated tool guide. The official authentication page provides the exact `GET /api/v2/deals` request. Signup is one opaque trial step, followed by public token and request instructions.

alternatives_considered.name: Insomnia API-token route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Insomnia is a documented peer and converges on the same token-authenticated request. Postman is listed first and has a direct collection authorization sequence.

alternatives_considered.name: Direct cURL request
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The authentication page shows a concise cURL example, but the dedicated first-request guide centers Postman and Insomnia.

alternatives_considered.name: OAuth app and Marketplace path
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Pipedrive sends app builders to OAuth. The API-token route is the shorter own-account path and avoids app registration and user installation before the first API response.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Pipedrive trial and company, retrieve the company-specific API token, and make the official direct cURL request to list deals.
assumed_prior_state: No Pipedrive user, company, company domain, trial, API permission, API token, deals, Postman installation, collection, or environment is assumed.
A supported computer, browser, email or Google account, and permission to install Postman are available.
No signup, Google authorization, reCAPTCHA, verification, company onboarding, API enablement, token retrieval, software installation, or API request was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Make the first request to the Pipedrive API with an API token.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: An authenticated `GET https://{companydomain}.pipedrive.com/api/v2/deals` request returns HTTP 200 and a JSON body whose `success` field is true, whether the data array contains deals or is empty.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The Deals endpoint reference shows a 200 OK response with `success: true`, `data`, and `additional_data`; business data is not required to prove API transport and authentication success.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: HTTP 200 with a JSON response whose `success` field is true.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the earliest observable terminal in the selected official quickstart. The workshop policy permits treating signup as one opaque step and then reconstructing the publicly documented post-login path.

prerequisites.requirement: Create or access a Pipedrive trial account and its company workspace.
Have API access enabled for the user; if the API tab is absent, follow the company API-enablement route.
Know the company domain used in the Pipedrive API base URL.
Retrieve the API token for the intended user and company from Personal preferences > API.
Install Postman and import or add the Pipedrive API collection.

primary_path.action: Open Pipedrive’s official API-token authentication guide.
primary_path.output: The direct cURL Deals request is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The guide shows the `x-api-token` header and endpoint.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: OAuth is required for distributable apps, not this own-account first request.

primary_path.action: Create the no-credit-card Pipedrive trial and company as one opaque signup step.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Pipedrive company workspace and company domain are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Complete any signup, verification, legal, company, and onboarding steps requested by Pipedrive; individual form fields are outside this record.

primary_path.action: Open account name > Company settings > Personal preferences > API and copy the user’s API token.
primary_path.output: The company-specific API token is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The API tab displays the token.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the API tab is absent, follow the documented API-enablement route.

primary_path.action: Run the documented cURL `GET https://{companydomain}.pipedrive.com/api/v2/deals` with the token in `x-api-token`.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Invalid domain, token, or API entitlement returns an error.

primary_path.output: The first Pipedrive API request is complete.
primary_path.success_signal: The response has `success: true`; `data` may be populated or empty.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Business data is not required for transport and authentication success.

branches.path: Enter a work email, accept the linked terms and privacy notice through signup, optionally choose marketing email, and pass reCAPTCHA before the hidden continuation.
branches.effect_on_first_success: May add email verification and account-detail steps that are not exposed publicly.

branches.path: Leave Pipedrive for Google authorization, select or authenticate an identity, then return to the hidden Pipedrive onboarding route.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Replaces the initial email form but adds an external identity-provider boundary.

branches.condition: The API tab is not visible
branches.path: Follow Pipedrive's enabling-API tutorial before attempting to retrieve the token.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an administrator or permission path before credentials are available.

branches.path: Import the v1 or v2 OpenAPI URL, generate the collection, select OpenAPI env, set `xApiToken`, and use the prefilled Auth values for a request.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes local setup but converges on the same authenticated API outcome.

branches.condition: Build a distributable app rather than an own-account integration
branches.path: Create an app and implement OAuth 2.0 instead of using the personal API token.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds Developer Hub registration, OAuth configuration, authorization, and installation concerns.

friction_gates.description: Email signup requires a work email and the public page is protected by reCAPTCHA.
Signing up accepts Pipedrive's Terms of Service and Privacy Notice.
Work email and Google are peer initial signup methods with different identity paths.
Company creation, authenticated onboarding, company domain, and settings convergence are not exposed by the public registration response.
API may be disabled for a company user, in which case the API tab is absent and must be enabled before token retrieval.
The API token is company-specific, exposes that user's data, and only one token can be active at a time.
The selected first-request route requires Postman plus a Pipedrive API collection and inherited header authentication.

sources.title: Authentication | Pipedrive Developer Documentation
sections_used: If you're working with an API token integration

excluded_after_success: Creating or mutating CRM records, pagination, rate-limit handling, building an OAuth app, Marketplace publication, token rotation, production secret storage, and multi-user integration behavior occur after the first authenticated Deals response.
